mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the signal predictive power reverses sign in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation remains elevated above 310.0 while the federal funds rate stays above 5.0%, the resulting real rate compression and policy uncertainty suppress equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of CPIAUCSL > 310.0 and FEDFUNDS > 5.0% yields positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, triggering corporate earnings downgrades and risk-off portfolio rebalancing that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 50% of confirmed recession quarters, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces retail sales growth, it signals improving inventory turnover and supply-side efficiency, which enhances corporate profitability and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The differential between INDPRO and RSAFS growth fails to predict 5-day returns with a positive coefficient, or the signal loses predictive power when inventory-to-sales ratios exceed historical medians.
